reasoning: The crowd assigned a ~1.1% probability to the EU importing 15 bcm of Russian gas in any quarter of 2025. Following the halt of Russian gas transit via Ukraine in January 2025, overall Russian pipeline flows to the EU dropped further. While the EU still imports some Russian pipeline gas (e.g. via TurkStream) and Russian LNG, reaching 15 bcm per quarter (which is roughly 60 bcm annualized) is highly improbable given that total Russian gas imports to the EU in 2023 and 2024 were already well below 15 bcm per quarter (around 10-12 bcm per quarter, or ~40-45 bcm annually). The end of the Ukraine transit route removes ~14-15 bcm of annual capacity (~3.5-4 bcm per quarter), meaning alternative routes would have to increase massively beyond capacity, which is contrary to the overall EU policy of phasing down Russian gas. I strongly agree with the extremely low base rate.
